extraction.one_line_summary: COQUARD Bryan won from a split peloton after Jayco-AlUla exploited crosswinds to fracture the field mid-stage
tactical_patterns: Jayco-AlUla premeditated wind split execution in Aldinga crosswind section fractured field before final circuits, eliminating climbers
INEOS wound up pace when 20-second gap threatened, revealing they prioritized group cohesion over stage win
Breakaway attempts by RICKAERT Jonas failed when peloton allowed intermediate sprint catch, forcing energy expenditure before wind split

rider_signals.COQUARD Bryan: positive: first World Tour win after 10-year career shows he can finish splits when pure sprinters eliminated
rider_signals.YATES Adam: neutral: maintained 15-second GC lead but wanted leadout support back, suggesting team stretched thin in split
rider_signals.VINE Jay: positive: held 15-second GC position despite mixing in sprint chaos, showing resilience in non-climber terrain
